diagrams.title: BST Insertion Process
diagrams.description: Shows step-by-step insertion of nodes (50, 30, 70, 20, 40, 60, 80) into a Binary Search Tree, demonstrating the comparison logic at each step - left subtree for smaller values, right subtree for larger values.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how BST maintains ordered structure through recursive insertion, showing that all left children < parent < all right children

diagrams.title: BST Deletion Cases
diagrams.description: Illustrates three deletion scenarios: (1) deleting a leaf node - simple removal, (2) deleting node with one child - promote child, (3) deleting node with two children - find inorder successor/replacement
elements: Case 2: Single child deletion (node 30 with child 25)
Case 3: Two children deletion (node 50 replaced by successor 60)
Arrows showing node connections before/after deletion
diagrams.purpose: Teaches the three distinct cases for removing nodes while preserving BST property, crucial for understanding tree maintenance

diagrams.title: BST Search Operation
diagrams.description: Demonstrates searching for values (25 and 75) in the BST, showing the path taken at each comparison - going left if target < current node, right if target > current node
elements: Search path for value 25 (50->30->20->null - not found)
Search path for value 75 (50->70->80->null - not found)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes the O(h) time complexity search algorithm, showing how BST enables efficient logarithmic search compared to linear structures
